dashboard.title: Your dashboard
dashboard.summary: Quick overview of your applications, documents, and messages.

overview.title: What you see here
overview.summary: Your dashboard shows your campers, your applications, document tasks, and recent messages.
overview.details: Each card on the page lets you jump straight to that section. The numbers update live as your application moves through review.

start_application.title: Starting a new application
start_application.summary: Click "Apply Now" or "New Application" to begin an application for a camper.
start_application.details: You can apply for more than one camper. Each camper gets their own separate application.

continue_draft.title: Continuing a saved draft
continue_draft.summary: Your work auto-saves, so you can close the page and come back anytime. Click a draft card to pick up where you left off.
continue_draft.details: Drafts are private — camp staff cannot see a draft until you submit it.

document_tasks.title: Document tasks
document_tasks.summary: If camp staff have requested a document, a "Pending Documents" alert will appear here. Click it to go straight to the upload page.
document_tasks.details: Required documents (immunization records, insurance cards, and medical exam forms) also appear on the Documents page.

inbox_link.title: Checking your messages
inbox_link.summary: Click "Inbox" in the sidebar or look in the Recent Activity section to see messages from camp staff.
inbox_link.details: All communication with camp happens through the inbox so there is a record of every conversation.

pending_meaning.question: What does "Pending" mean?
pending_meaning.answer: Pending means your application has been submitted and is waiting for a staff member to review it. No action is needed on your part — we will reach out if anything is missing.

multiple_campers.question: Can I apply for more than one camper?
multiple_campers.answer: Yes. Start a new application for each camper. Each application is tracked separately so nothing gets mixed up.

previous_year.question: Why don't I see last year's application?
previous_year.answer: Previous applications are archived once the session is over. Your current dashboard shows only active or recent applications. Contact camp staff if you need a copy of an old application.

documents_waiting.message: You have {{count}} document request(s) waiting to upload.

draft_in_progress.message: You have an application draft in progress.

no_applications.message: Welcome! Start your first application when you are ready.

campers.title: Your campers
campers.body: This card shows how many campers are registered on your account. Each camper can have their own separate application.

pending.title: Pending applications
pending.body: This card counts applications that are submitted and waiting for a staff member to review. No action is needed while an application is pending.

draft.title: In-progress draft
draft.body: When you have a draft application in progress, a banner appears here. Click Continue to pick up right where you left off.

start-application.title: Start an application
start-application.body: Click this button to begin a new application for a camper. You can apply for multiple campers — each gets their own application.

activity.title: Recent activity
activity.body: Your most recent messages, application updates, and document requests appear here. Click any row to go directly to that item.

applicationForm.title: Filling out the application
applicationForm.summary: How to complete each section, save your progress, and submit when ready.

sections_overview.title: Sections at a glance
sections_overview.summary: The application has 11 sections. The sidebar shows your progress and highlights which sections still need attention.
sections_overview.details: You do not have to finish in one sitting. Come back anytime — your work is saved automatically.

autosave.title: Your work saves automatically
autosave.summary: Every few seconds your answers are saved. You can close the page and come back to your draft without losing anything.
autosave.details: You will see a small "Saved" indicator near the top of the page after each automatic save.

section_complete.title: When a section turns green
section_complete.summary: A section turns green when all required fields are filled. For sections like behavior, equipment, diet, and medications, you can also check "Nothing to declare" to mark them complete.
section_complete.details: "Nothing to declare" means you are confirming that there is nothing to report for that topic — not that you skipped it.

documents.title: Uploading documents
documents.summary: Section 11 is where you upload required documents. You can also upload them later from the Documents page if you do not have them ready.
documents.details: Required documents include an immunization record, insurance card, and medical exam form. All three must be on file before your application can be fully approved.

signature.title: Adding your signature
signature.summary: Section 10 asks for your signature. You can draw it with your mouse or finger, or just type your name — either option is accepted.
signature.details: The typed name is converted to a signed image automatically. You do not need any special software.

submit.title: Submitting your application
submit.summary: Once all sections are green, the Submit button becomes active. Click it to send your application to camp staff. You cannot edit an application after submitting.
submit.details: If you need to make changes after submitting, you can withdraw the application and start a new one.

yellow_section.question: Can I submit if a section is still yellow?
yellow_section.answer: No. All sections must show green before the Submit button becomes active. Check the sidebar to see which sections still need information.

nothing_to_declare.question: What does "Nothing to declare" mean?
nothing_to_declare.answer: It means you are confirming that the topic does not apply to your camper. For example, checking "Nothing to declare" in the Medications section means your camper takes no regular medications.

edit_after_submit.question: Can I make changes after submitting?
edit_after_submit.answer: No, submitted applications are locked. If you need to make a correction, contact camp staff or withdraw the application and submit a new one.

documents.title: Your documents
documents.summary: Upload required and requested documents, and track their status.

two_kinds.title: Two kinds of documents
two_kinds.summary: Required documents are needed for every applicant. Requested documents are ones that a staff member has specifically asked you to provide.
two_kinds.details: Both types appear on this page. Required documents have named slots (immunization record, insurance card, medical exam form). Requested documents appear as cards with the staff member's instructions.

upload_required.title: Uploading required documents
upload_required.summary: Click the slot for the document you want to upload, then choose a file from your device.
upload_required.details: Accepted file types are PDF, JPG, PNG, and WebP. If the file is too large or the wrong type, you will see an error message.

upload_requested.title: Responding to a document request
upload_requested.summary: When a staff member requests a specific document, a card appears here. Click "Upload" on that card to attach your file.
upload_requested.details: The card shows any instructions the staff member included, such as which page of a form to use or what information should be visible.

submit_to_staff.title: Submitting a document for review
submit_to_staff.summary: After uploading, a document starts as a draft. Click the orange "Submit to Staff" button to send it for review.
submit_to_staff.details: Documents stay as drafts until you submit them. Staff cannot see a draft document, so make sure to click Submit when you are ready.

status.title: Document statuses
status.summary: Pending means it has been submitted and is waiting for review. Approved means it is accepted. Rejected means there was a problem — the reason is shown and you can re-upload.
status.details: If a document is rejected, read the reason shown on the card. Common reasons include a blurry photo, a cropped image that cut off important information, or an expired document.

file_types.question: What file types can I upload?
file_types.answer: You can upload PDF, JPG, PNG, and WebP files. If you have a physical document, take a clear photo with your phone and upload the image.

rejected_document.question: Why was my document rejected?
rejected_document.answer: The rejection reason is shown on the document card. The most common reasons are a photo that is too small or blurry to read, or an image that cuts off part of the document. Re-take the photo in good lighting and re-upload.

requests_awaiting.message: You have {{count}} document request(s) awaiting upload.

documents_rejected.message: {{count}} document(s) were rejected. Re-upload to continue.

documents_pending.message: {{count}} required document(s) still need to be uploaded.

requested.title: Documents requested from you
requested.body: When a staff member asks for a specific document, it appears here as a task card. Upload your file and click Submit to respond.

submitted.title: Submitted documents
submitted.body: Files you have already sent to camp staff appear here. You can see the current review status — pending, approved, or rejected — for each one.

upload.title: Additional uploads
upload.body: Use this area to upload any extra documents that were not specifically requested — for example, a secondary insurance card or a letter from a doctor.

applicationDetail.title: Your application
applicationDetail.summary: Track your application's status and what happens next.

status_meaning.title: What the status means
status_meaning.summary: Draft means not submitted. Submitted means received by camp. Under Review means a staff member is looking at it. Approved or Rejected is the final decision. Waitlisted means there is no open spot right now.
status_meaning.details: Withdrawn means you cancelled the application yourself. If you withdraw, you can apply again for the same or a different session.

submitted_what_next.title: After you submit
submitted_what_next.summary: Once submitted, a staff member will review your application. This can take a few days depending on the time of year.
submitted_what_next.details: You will receive a message in your inbox when there is an update. Keep an eye on your inbox and make sure your documents are uploaded.

under_review.title: Under review
under_review.summary: Under review means a staff member has opened your application and is working through it. They may message you to ask for additional documents.
under_review.details: You do not need to do anything while your application is under review unless staff reach out.

approved.title: Approved
approved.summary: Your application has been approved. Check your inbox for a message with next steps, including payment and arrival details.
approved.details: Approval means your camper has a confirmed spot. Follow any instructions in the message to complete enrollment.

withdraw.title: Withdrawing an application
withdraw.summary: You can withdraw an application at any time before it is approved. Once withdrawn, it cannot be undone — you would need to start a new application.
withdraw.details: Contact camp staff if you want to withdraw after approval. Staff withdrawals are handled case by case.

review_time.question: How long does review take?
review_time.answer: Review times vary depending on the time of year and how many applications are in queue. Check your inbox for updates — staff will message you if they need anything.

waitlisted.question: What happens if I am waitlisted?
waitlisted.answer: Being waitlisted means there is no open spot right now, but you are in line. If another applicant withdraws, you may be offered a spot. You will be notified by inbox message if that happens.

draft_not_submitted.message: Your application is saved as a draft. Submit it when you are ready.

awaiting_review.message: Your application is submitted and awaiting review.

under_review.message: A staff member is reviewing your application.

approved.message: Approved! Check your inbox for next steps.

rejected.message: Your application was not accepted. See the notes section for details.

waitlisted.message: You are on the waitlist. We will message you if a spot opens up.

status.title: Status timeline
status.body: This card shows where your application is in the review process. The timeline moves from Submitted to Under Review to Approved as staff work through it.

actions.title: Actions
actions.body: These buttons let you withdraw an application or apply for a new session. The withdraw option is only shown for applications that are still active.

inbox.title: Your inbox
inbox.summary: Read messages from camp staff and reply to document requests.

reading.title: Reading a message
reading.summary: Click any row in the inbox list to open the conversation. Messages appear in chronological order.
reading.details: Unread messages are highlighted. Once you open a thread it is marked as read automatically.

reply.title: Replying to camp staff
reply.summary: Type your reply in the box at the bottom of the conversation and click Send.
reply.details: Replies go directly to the staff member who sent the original message. You can attach a document to your reply if needed.

archive.title: Archiving a thread
archive.summary: To clean up your inbox, select a thread and click Archive. Archived threads move to the Archived folder and can be found there at any time.
archive.details: Archiving a thread does not delete it and does not affect your application. It is just a way to keep your inbox tidy.

document_requests_in_inbox.title: Document requests in messages
document_requests_in_inbox.summary: Some messages include a link to upload a document. Click the link in the message to go straight to the upload page for that request.
document_requests_in_inbox.details: These messages are sent when a staff member needs a specific file from you. Uploading through the link connects your file to the right request automatically.

system_messages.question: Why is a message marked "System"?
system_messages.answer: System messages are automatic notifications — for example, a confirmation when your application status changes or a reminder about an upcoming deadline. You do not need to reply to system messages.

direct_email.question: Can I contact camp staff by email instead?
direct_email.answer: Please use the inbox so that all communication is tracked in one place. This makes it easier for staff to help you and keeps a record of every conversation.

paperForms.title: Paper Application Forms
paperForms.summary: How to download, complete, and upload paper application forms for camp.

hero.title: Paper application process
hero.body: This page guides you through submitting a paper application — download blank forms, print and complete them, then upload scanned copies for staff review.

steps-area.title: Four-step workflow
steps-area.body: The process has four steps: register your camper and session, download the blank forms, upload your completed scans, then submit everything to staff.

what_is_paper.title: What is a paper application?
what_is_paper.summary: A paper application lets you complete camp forms by hand instead of online.
what_is_paper.details: Some families prefer paper forms, or a doctor may need to complete a medical section in writing. This page manages that entire process — from downloading the blank forms to uploading your signed copies.

getting_started.title: Getting started (Step 0)
getting_started.summary: Select the camp session and the camper you are applying for.
getting_started.details: Before you can access the forms, you need to link this application to a specific session and camper. If your camper is already in the system, select them from the list. If this is a new camper, enter their name and date of birth to create their record.

download_forms.title: Download blank forms (Step 1)
download_forms.summary: Download the official forms, print them, and fill them in by hand.
download_forms.details: The main packet includes the application form and medical health history. Supplemental forms (CPAP waiver, seizure action plan, G-tube feeding plan) are only needed if they apply to your camper. Print, complete, and sign all relevant forms before moving on.

upload_forms.title: Upload completed forms (Step 2)
upload_forms.summary: Scan or photograph your completed forms and upload them here.
upload_forms.details: Each form has its own upload slot. You can upload a PDF, JPG, or PNG. The Application Packet and Medical Form are required — supplemental slots are optional and only appear if relevant. Uploading saves your file but does not notify staff yet.

submit.title: Submit for review (Step 3)
submit.summary: Click Submit to send your uploaded forms to camp staff.
submit.details: Once all required forms are uploaded, use the Submit button to formally send them for review. Staff will not see your uploads until you submit. After submitting, you can still upload additional optional forms — they will be reviewed alongside your application.

why_paper.question: Can I switch to the online application instead?
why_paper.answer: Yes. The online digital application is on the Applications page. The paper route is an alternative — you do not have to choose one or the other for all campers. Each application can use a different submission method.

multiple_campers.question: Can I submit paper forms for more than one camper?
multiple_campers.answer: Yes. Each camper needs their own application. Use the "Start New Application" button to begin a second paper application, then switch between them using the application cards at the top of the page.

upload_vs_submit.question: What is the difference between uploading and submitting?
upload_vs_submit.answer: Uploading saves your file to our system but keeps it as a draft — staff cannot see it yet. Submitting sends all your uploads to staff for review. You must click Submit after uploading for your application to move forward.
